rationale: In the conceptual background, the citing paper uses Lee and Labroo (2004) to support its mechanism claim that matching conditions can create conceptual processing fluency. That logic is then carried directly into H4, where the authors predict that construal-matched search content increases fluency, which improves search satisfaction and perceived goal progress. Although the citation appears alongside other fluency references, it is used to motivate a core mediating process rather than as a purely passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the cited paper's conceptual-fluency insight to online search, arguing that matching consumer construal with search/ad language should increase processing fluency. It then tests this fluency pathway as a mediator of search satisfaction and perceived goal progress.
